_source: ISPRL Vizag to HPCL Vizag refinery: direct pipeline link (emergency release). Max flow = SPR max discharge 85,000 bbl/day [ESTIMATED]. High cost $8.00/bbl ensures solver only uses SPR when cheaper routes cannot meet demand — serves as last-resort cost signal, not an economic value.
ISPRL Mangalore to MRPL Mangalore: pipeline link. 95,000 bbl/day [ESTIMATED].
ISPRL Padur to BPCL Kochi: pipeline (Padur is ~200km north of Kochi, pipeline exists per MoPNG). 160,000 bbl/day [ESTIMATED].
ISPRL Padur can also feed MRPL Mangalore (closer facility). Shared capacity with Kochi pipe; total Padur discharge capped at 160,000 bbl/day across both edges combined.
Virtual connector. Capacity = this source's export ceiling, so that the max-flow baseline respects it (NetworkX max-flow reads edge capacities only, never node capacity_bbl_day).

Salaya-Mathura crude pipeline (SMPL), IndianOil. 2,663 km total / 25 MMTPA installed; Koyali is the first delivery point. Capacity is shared with the Mathura and Panipat offtakes and is enforced at the port_salaya node. Tariff = $0.75/1000km x 1,130 km [ESTIMATED from PNGRB-regulated long-haul crude tariffs]. Transit = 1,130 km at 130 km/day (~1.5 m/s design velocity) [ESTIMATED]. Generated by scripts/build_network.py -- do not hand-edit.

Salaya-Mathura crude pipeline (SMPL), IndianOil. Mathura is the pipeline's namesake terminus at roughly 1,900 km along the route. Tariff = $0.75/1000km x 1,900 km [ESTIMATED from PNGRB-regulated long-haul crude tariffs]. Transit = 1,900 km at 130 km/day (~1.5 m/s design velocity) [ESTIMATED]. Generated by scripts/build_network.py -- do not hand-edit.

Salaya-Mathura crude pipeline (SMPL) extension to Panipat, IndianOil. 2,663 km end to end. Tariff = $0.75/1000km x 2,663 km [ESTIMATED from PNGRB-regulated long-haul crude tariffs]. Transit = 2,663 km at 130 km/day (~1.5 m/s design velocity) [ESTIMATED]. Generated by scripts/build_network.py -- do not hand-edit.

Mundra-Panipat crude pipeline (MPPL), IndianOil. 1,194 km / 8.4 MMTPA. A second 17.5 MMTPA line is under construction and is NOT modelled. Tariff = $0.75/1000km x 1,194 km [ESTIMATED from PNGRB-regulated long-haul crude tariffs]. Transit = 1,194 km at 130 km/day (~1.5 m/s design velocity) [ESTIMATED]. Generated by scripts/build_network.py -- do not hand-edit.

Mundra-Bathinda crude pipeline, HPCL-Mittal Pipelines. 1,017 km, published capacity 11.25 MMTPA and expandable to 18 MMTPA; carried here at the refinery's own 11.3 MMTPA because the 0.05 MMTPA difference is below the precision of either published figure and would otherwise strand the refinery at 99.6% in perpetuity. Sole crude supply to the Guru Gobind Singh refinery. Tariff = $0.75/1000km x 1,017 km [ESTIMATED from PNGRB-regulated long-haul crude tariffs]. Transit = 1,017 km at 130 km/day (~1.5 m/s design velocity) [ESTIMATED]. Generated by scripts/build_network.py -- do not hand-edit.

Mumbai High (ONGC Western Offshore) to the BPCL Mumbai refinery via the Uran terminal. Domestic crude. Tariff = $0.75/1000km x 180 km [ESTIMATED from PNGRB-regulated long-haul crude tariffs]. Transit = 180 km at 130 km/day (~1.5 m/s design velocity) [ESTIMATED]. Generated by scripts/build_network.py -- do not hand-edit.

Mumbai High (ONGC Western Offshore) to the HPCL Mumbai refinery via the Uran terminal. Domestic crude. Field capacity is shared with the adjacent BPCL offtake and is enforced at the source node. Tariff = $0.75/1000km x 180 km [ESTIMATED from PNGRB-regulated long-haul crude tariffs]. Transit = 180 km at 130 km/day (~1.5 m/s design velocity) [ESTIMATED]. Generated by scripts/build_network.py -- do not hand-edit.

ONGC Gujarat onshore fields (Ankleshwar / Gandhar / Kalol) to the Koyali refinery, which was originally built around this production. Domestic crude. Tariff = $0.75/1000km x 120 km [ESTIMATED from PNGRB-regulated long-haul crude tariffs]. Transit = 120 km at 130 km/day (~1.5 m/s design velocity) [ESTIMATED]. Generated by scripts/build_network.py -- do not hand-edit.

Vadinar-Bina crude pipeline, BORL/BPCL. 937 km / 7.8 MMTPA. Sole crude supply to the Bina refinery. Tariff = $0.75/1000km x 937 km [ESTIMATED from PNGRB-regulated long-haul crude tariffs]. Transit = 937 km at 130 km/day (~1.5 m/s design velocity) [ESTIMATED]. Generated by scripts/build_network.py -- do not hand-edit.

Short in-port connection from the Paradip crude terminal to the adjacent IOCL Paradip refinery. Tariff = $0.75/1000km x 10 km [ESTIMATED from PNGRB-regulated long-haul crude tariffs]. Transit = 10 km at 130 km/day (~1.5 m/s design velocity) [ESTIMATED]. Generated by scripts/build_network.py -- do not hand-edit.

Paradip-Haldia-Barauni crude pipeline (PHBPL), IndianOil. 1,465 km / 20.4 MMTPA; Haldia is the first inland delivery point. Haldia also receives crude directly by sea at Haldia Dock, so both routes exist. Tariff = $0.75/1000km x 330 km [ESTIMATED from PNGRB-regulated long-haul crude tariffs]. Transit = 330 km at 130 km/day (~1.5 m/s design velocity) [ESTIMATED]. Generated by scripts/build_network.py -- do not hand-edit.

Paradip-Haldia-Barauni crude pipeline (PHBPL), IndianOil. Barauni is the terminus at 1,465 km. Tariff = $0.75/1000km x 1,465 km [ESTIMATED from PNGRB-regulated long-haul crude tariffs]. Transit = 1,465 km at 130 km/day (~1.5 m/s design velocity) [ESTIMATED]. Generated by scripts/build_network.py -- do not hand-edit.

Bonny to the Suez Canal via Gibraltar and the Mediterranean. Longer and dearer than the Cape route for India-bound cargoes, so the optimizer only reaches for it when the Cape is constrained. Distance 9,923 km (searoute). Cost = fixed $1.2214 + $0.1341/1000km x 9,923 km. Transit = 9,923 km at 13 kn laden. Generated by scripts/build_network.py -- do not hand-edit.

Suez Canal transit and Red Sea crossing to Bab-el-Mandeb. Carries the Suez Canal Authority toll and one day of scheduled convoy transit. Distance 2,334 km (searoute).
